description: Implement a hidden multi-project/account system where each project has isolated workspaces, its own password, and all project management (create, switch, delete) is done through secret/hidden interactions invisible to casual observers.
steps: Step 1 - Data layer for projects: Add new state and localStorage structure for multiple projects. Each project has: { id, name, password, workspaces, activeTab, nextId }. Store the full project list under a non-obvious localStorage key (e.g., 'nexus-app-state' or similar). Store the active project ID separately. The default state should have one project containing the current workspaces data. On first load, migrate existing localStorage data (premium-workspaces, premium-active-tab, premium-counter, nexus-password, nexus-password-enabled) into the new project structure, then clean up old keys.

Step 2 - Secret activation mechanism: Add a hidden keyboard shortcut to open the project management panel. Use Ctrl+Shift+P (or similar non-conflicting combo) to open a secret modal/overlay. This modal should have no visible trigger - no button, no menu item, no hint anywhere in the UI. The shortcut listener should be added at the component level via useEffect with a keydown handler.

Step 3 - Secret project management modal: When the secret shortcut is triggered, show a minimal, non-descript modal (styled to look like a system prompt, not like a feature panel). This modal should support: (a) Creating a new project (asks for project name + password), (b) Switching to a different project (shows list, requires password entry for selected project), (c) Deleting a project (requires password confirmation), (d) The modal can be dismissed with Escape or clicking outside. The modal should not have any title/header that reveals 'projects' or 'accounts' - keep it cryptic (e.g., just show a password input that leads to the right project).

Step 4 - Project switching logic: When user switches projects via the secret modal, save the current project state, load the new project's workspaces/activeTab/nextId into the main app state. The app should seamlessly show the new project's workspaces. The password gate (existing system) should use the current project's password. Each project's password is independent.

Step 5 - Stealth design: Ensure there is absolutely NO visual indicator that multiple projects exist. The sidebar workspace list, header dropdown, import/export - everything only shows/operates on the current project's data. Remove the existing 'Password Protection' section from the sidebar (since password is now set per-project during creation via the secret panel). The existing password gate still functions - if the current project has a password set, it shows the lock screen on load. The password enable/disable and change should move to the secret project panel.

Step 6 - Alternative secret access method: In addition to the keyboard shortcut, add a secret click pattern for mobile users - triple-tap on the app logo (the Network icon in the header) within 1 second to open the project management panel. This gives mobile users a way to access the hidden system.

Step 7 - Project data isolation: Ensure import/export only operates on the current project's data. The exported JSON should not contain any reference to other projects. Clipboard operations (copy/cut/paste nodes) should work within a project's workspaces but not leak data between projects.
acceptance_criteria: Running `npm run build` passes without errors
The app loads with existing data migrated into a default project - no data loss
Pressing Ctrl+Shift+P opens a secret project management panel with no visible UI trigger
Triple-tapping the app logo in the header also opens the secret panel (mobile access)
A new project can be created with a name and password from the secret panel
Switching projects requires entering the target project's password
Each project has completely isolated workspaces, nodes, edges, and groups
No visual element (button, menu item, text, icon) in the normal UI reveals the existence of multiple projects
The existing password lock gate works using the current project's password
The 'Password Protection' section is removed from the visible sidebar
Import/export only operates on the current project's data
The secret panel can be dismissed with Escape key or clicking outside
verification: Run `npm run build` in /projects/sandbox/mind-map2 and confirm it completes successfully with no errors
